key_insight: Danus coordinates multiple math-reasoning agents around a shared fact graph, turning verified intermediate claims into organized long-horizon proof memory.
engineering_relevance: The design offers a practical orchestration pattern for scaling agentic theorem-proving systems while preserving traceability, consistency, and human reviewability.
method: Main agent plans, summarizes, and redirects proof search across workers.
Worker agents explore proof directions in parallel.
A stateless verifier checks claims before adding them, with each fact stored alongside proof and dependencies in the fact graph.
evidence_signal: The paper evaluates Danus on six research-level case studies spanning algebraic geometry, singularity theory, and combinatorics.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliable verification and dependency management; errors or bottlenecks there could weaken the shared fact graph and limit scalability.
paper_title: Danus: Orchestrating Mathematical Reasoning Agents with Fact-Graph Memory
